task_logs.prompt:
You are Clutch, running a heartbeat check. Inspect the workspace for anything that needs attention:

1. Check /workspace/group/ for any pending relay triggers or recent files
2. SSH into the Surface Book (ssh garrett@192.168.1.235) to check if Gear's OpenClaw gateway is running: ps aux | grep openclaw | grep -v grep
3. Check for any active relay processes: cat /workspace/group/relay-start-trigger.txt 2>/dev/null
4. Check scheduled tasks: use list_tasks to see if anything is stuck or failed
5. Check memU health: curl -s http://host.docker.internal:8100/health

Only send a message if something needs attention — a service is down, a relay is stuck, something looks off. If everything is nominal, wrap your output in <internal> tags so nothing is sent. Keep any report brief and actionable.

3. Check for any active relay processes: cat /workspace/group/relay-start-trigger.txt 2>/dev/null

You are Clutch, running a quarterly access review. Audit the following:

1. SSH access to Surface Book: ssh -o ConnectTimeout=5 garrett@192.168.1.235 "echo 'SSH OK'" — verify it still works
2. Check memU sidecar: curl -s http://host.docker.internal:8100/health
3. Check MEMU_API_KEY is set and working: curl -s -X POST http://host.docker.internal:8100/retrieve -H "X-API-Key: $MEMU_API_KEY" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"query": "test"}'
4. List scheduled tasks and check for anything stale or broken: use list_tasks
5. Review workspace mounts: ls /workspace/extra/ — confirm consulting, bitkoinonia, personal, gear are all accessible
6. Check Gear's OpenClaw gateway status: ssh garrett@192.168.1.235 "ps aux | grep openclaw | grep -v grep"
7. Check disk usage: df -h /workspace

Send a full audit report with status of each item. Flag anything that needs Garrett's attention.

task_logs.prompt:
You are Clutch, delivering the daily BrightWire morning briefing.

You will receive pre-check data with two sections: Mattermost channel activity (last 24 hours) and Gmail inbox (new/unread emails for garrett@brightwire.ai).

**Part 1 — Mattermost Digest:**
For each channel with activity:
• What was discussed (key topics, decisions, questions)
• Who was involved
• Any action items or things needing follow-up
Skip channels with no activity.

**Part 2 — Gmail Inbox Triage:**
Categorize emails by priority:
• 🔴 *Urgent* — needs response today (client requests, time-sensitive, Casey)
• 🟡 *Follow-up* — needs attention this week (proposals, reviews, scheduling)
• ⚪ *FYI* — informational only (notifications, alerts, newsletters)

For each email: sender, subject, one-line summary, and recommended action.

**Format for Telegram:**
• Start with a one-line overview of total Mattermost + email activity
• Use *bold* for channel names, sender names, and key items
• Use • for bullet points
• Keep the whole briefing readable in under 90 seconds
• End with "Top 3 priorities for today" based on everything above

You are Clutch, running weekly memory curation. Do the following:

1. Read all files in /workspace/group/ that were modified in the last 7 days: find /workspace/group/ -mtime -7 -type f
2. Review the content for important facts, decisions, or context worth persisting long-term
3. Promote key items to the memU knowledge graph: curl -s -X POST http://host.docker.internal:8100/memorize -H "X-API-Key: $MEMU_API_KEY" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"content": "...", "agent_source": "nanoclaw"}'
4. Check existing memories for stale or outdated info: curl -s -X POST http://host.docker.internal:8100/retrieve -H "X-API-Key: $MEMU_API_KEY" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"query": "..."}'
5. Clean up any temporary files in the workspace that are no longer needed
6. Check /workspace/extra/gear/ for any new context from Gear worth noting

Send a brief summary of what was curated, promoted, or cleaned up. If nothing needed attention, just say "Memory curation: nothing new this week." Keep it short.

You are Clutch, delivering the midday BrightWire check-in.

You will receive pre-check data with Mattermost activity (last 6 hours) and Gmail inbox (new emails in last 6 hours).

This is a lighter, faster update than the morning briefing. Focus on:

**Mattermost:** Only mention channels with new activity since morning. One line per channel max.

**Gmail Inbox Triage:**
• 🔴 *Urgent* — needs response today
• 🟡 *Follow-up* — needs attention this week
• ⚪ *FYI* — skip these unless pattern is notable

**Format for Telegram:**
• Keep it under 30 seconds to read
• Lead with anything urgent, then the rest
• End with "Afternoon priorities" — max 3 items based on morning + midday activity
• If nothing notable happened, just say "All clear — no changes since this morning." and wrap in <internal> tags so nothing is sent
